title: South Australian Police Reveal Some Of Gus Lamont’s Family Members Are Not Co-Operating | 10 News
description: There has been an update in the Gus Lamont case, from the state's top cop, about police interactions with the family. Police Commissioner Grant Stevens has revealed that two family members of Gus Lamont are only communicating via their legal representatives and not directly with investigators seeking to find the missing four-year-old. title: Queensland Police Shoot Alleged Armed Man On Motorway | 10 News
description: For the second time in two days, police have drawn their weapons and fired at an alleged offender in the southeast. This afternoon on the Sunshine Coast their gunshots ended the pursuit of a gunman who brought chaos to the Sunshine motorway. This is the second police shooting in Queensland in as many days. 10 News can reveal the first at Tingalpa yesterday, which almost claimed the life of an officer. title: Panic Petrol Buying, Annual Leave Increases, Kyle And Jackie O's Next Move, US-Iran | 10 News +
description: A service station in the Gold Coast has become the first station to run out of unleaded after panic petrol buying has started across the country as Aussies fear shortages and price hikes.  Aussies could soon see their annual leave increased after a new push to bring the minimum entitlement in line with the rest of the world. It comes as productivity continues to decline across the country but not everyone thinks it’s a good idea. description: The raging conflict in the Middle East has seen billions wiped off Asia’s stock markets, with South Korea’s index suffering its largest drop on record. title: Over 100 Missing, 78 Injured After Suspected Submarine Sinks Iranian Ship | 10 News
description: At least 78 people have been injured and over 100 are still missing after a suspected submarine attack on an Iranian warship.  Around 30 sailors have been rescued off the coast of Sri Lanka after the Iris Dena issued a distress call.  It’s not clear who may have carried out the attack.
